Patch Set 2:

Somehow this patch missed getting refreshed?  The dependency chain is a bit confusing (if there are any dependencies at all), because it think the patch order changed at some point in the past. In any case, this patch is the most important /sysfs patch remaining, and it would be great if we could still get this into 2.8 so that the master user tools also work with kernel clients.

Patch Set 3:

I see what is causing the failures. param_display() is not sending back the error code from read_param() or write_param(). For read_param we can probably ignore the error code since if we are reading more than one parameter at a time we want to continue and just report a error code. For write_param since it is setup to write one single parameter at a time we need to break and return with the error code.

Patch Set 8:

Have been testing this patch with the upstream client and it found a bug. force_lbug hangs when running lctl get_param and writing to one of the files in /sys/kernel/debug/lnet causes the lustre client to crash hard. Sanity test 401 fails due to duplicate directory in the upstream client. Both /sys/kernel/lustre and /sys/fs/lustre contain almost the same exact trees. Because of this the find command will repot some directories twice. So the find results need to stripped of $proc_dir at the start of the returned strings and then only the unique values need to be reported. So much more bash magic is still needed.

parents.subject: LU-7715 out: fix err_serious in out_handle

commit.subject: LU-5030 util: migrate lctl params functions to use cfs_get_paths()
commit.message:
LU-5030 util: migrate lctl params functions to use cfs_get_paths()

Make the normal lctl set_param,list_param, and get_param
operations to use the new cfs_get_paths() function which
enables sysfs support along side procfs.

Change-Id: I5817e96c3172de53930776f0891f2a642907bfde
Signed-off-by: Dmitry Eremin <dmitry.eremin@intel.com>
Signed-off-by: James Simmons <uja.ornl@yahoo.com>
Signed-off-by: Wang Chao <chao.ornl@gmail.com>
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/17466
Reviewed-by: Ryan Haasken <haasken@cray.com>
Tested-by: Jenkins
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: John L. Hammond <john.hammond@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <andreas.dilger@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
